reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss (No evidence of Silver Guest)

vision_report.inventory_reconciliation: The registry is largely inaccurate regarding current occupancy. P2 and P4 are absent; the pots appear to contain only soil and debris.

plant_audit.P1: String of Nickels: Appears dormant or absent; pot is dark and lacks visible foliage.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos: Single leaf remaining, showing signs of chlorosis and structural weakness. White Rabbit anchor is present.
plant_audit.P2_P4_Zone: Empty black pot contains only soil and eggshell fragments, indicating a failed propagation or loss of specimen.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp; no signs of active moss or fungal blooms.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in the black pot, likely an attempt at soil amendment or pest deterrent.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no significant organic matter or spills.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t5: Initial state shows a single Pothos leaf and a small sprout in the black pot.
temporal_deltas.t4_to_t2: Sprout in the black pot shows signs of wilting/necrosis.
temporal_deltas.t1_to_current: The sprout has effectively collapsed or withered into the soil; the Pothos leaf in P3 remains static but shows no growth.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of decline. The Pothos (P3) is the only surviving specimen, and it is struggling. The lack of light (North window only) and potential over-saturation of soil are likely contributors to the loss of P2 and P4.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a failing botanical environment. The Pothos in the rabbit-anchored pot is the sole survivor, though it appears stressed. The other pots are effectively barren, suggesting a failure in the propagation or maintenance cycle. The presence of eggshells suggests an amateur attempt to bolster soil nutrients, which has not yielded positive results.
